steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence begins with "By supplying," an introductory prepositional phrase that sets up context before the main clause "the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew" begins.

steps.title: Apply the introductory phrase rule
steps.content:
Left of blank: the introductory phrase "By supplying grain, textiles, and tin to societies that lacked these valuable ___". Right of blank: the main clause "Neo-Assyrian Empire grew not only in wealth but also in power." A comma separates an introductory phrase from the main clause.
  • Choice A — period: "By supplying grain, textiles, and tin to societies that lacked these valuable items. The Neo-Assyrian Empire grew..." — the period turns "By supplying..." into a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — no punctuation: "...lacked these valuable items the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew..." — the phrase runs directly into the main clause with no signal of where it ends. ✗
  • Choice C — semicolon: "...lacked these valuable items; the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew..." — a semicolon requires two independent clauses; "By supplying..." is a phrase, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — comma: "...lacked these valuable items, the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew..." — the comma cleanly closes the introductory phrase and opens the main clause. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
